posts.title: Rethinking Startup Funding in Morocco & North Africa
posts.excerpt: A customized seven-stage funding model tailored to Morocco and North Africa's startup ecosystem. Rather than directly adopting Western funding structures, success depends less on late-stage funding and more on strengthening our early stages — helping more startups reach product-market fit before scaling.

posts.title: A Champion
posts.excerpt: The story of Nora, a Moroccan international beach volleyball player and African champion — four international gold medals earned against negativity, injury, and zero financial support. A real-life sports film, told from an event hosted by Accelab Morocco.

posts.title: Discipline in the Little Things
content: “If you’re not disciplined in the little things, you won’t be disciplined in the big things.” — Warren Buffett
Most people think success comes from one brilliant idea or a massive breakthrough. Buffett built an empire by doing the opposite: mastering the boring, daily stuff.
He understood probability and risk. The formula looks like this: Expected Value = (probability of gain × amount gained) − (probability of loss × amount lost).
Easy → the goal is to avoid bets when the expected value is negative. So for every $10 bet, you can expect to lose practically all of it. The negative expected value makes it a horrible bet.

You can take this a step further and consider the opportunity cost of betting on numerous losing propositions instead of winning propositions over time. And that’s why Buffett is richer than all of us.
The goal of investing is to avoid permanent losses and bet when the odds are in your favor.
“Price is what you pay. Value is what you get.” — Warren Buffett

posts.title: 5 Key Points from an Early-Stage Venture Capital
content: ➤ Investors at early stage invest in people. It’s not just about the idea — VCs back founders they believe in. Passion, vision, and resilience often matter more than the product itself in the early days.
➤ Love the problem. Yes, love the problem, not your solution — that’s what VCs want you to focus on. Personally I don’t agree with this. I love my solutions 🙂

➤ Investors like to back something they can touch. While tech startups dominate conversations, they mentioned how one of their favorite investments was a bread 🍞 production company. Sometimes, the best opportunities are in tangible projects that you can touch.

➤ Startups beget startups. Ambitious founders often go on to create new ventures. A friend of mine, who was part of a startup, recently launched an impressive new project — further proof of how innovation fuels itself.
➤ Kindness can manifest into your surroundings. ↳ Let this one speak for itself.

posts.title: Perseverance and Staying Afloat
content: Recently, I had the honor of attending a session by Dr. Ayman Ismail, where he shared his insights into the experiences of startups in MENA and how to build scalable ventures in our region.
One particular case surprised me personally because I follow a similar approach in my own thinking. I asked Dr. Ayman a simple question: why didn’t the founder take investments to keep going?

His response was that the founder wasn’t satisfied with the terms offered by investors and kept holding out for a better deal. Unfortunately, no one was willing to take that big of a risk, and the startup began to sink.

Maybe in some cases, accepting what’s offered — even if it’s less than ideal — can provide the lifeline needed to extend the journey long enough to find success. Perhaps, had the founder taken that investment, the company might have become a unicorn.
This insight deeply resonated with me and reminded me that sometimes, perseverance and staying afloat are the best strategies we can adopt.
Thank you, STARTGATE, for hosting such an inspiring session, and Dr. Ayman Ismail for sharing your experiences and lessons with us!
